title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Poirot and Mrs. Oliver discuss the character of Roya Drake, a dominating woman who effectively runs the community, and the circumstances surrounding the death of thirteen-year-old Joyce Reynolds. Mrs. Oliver notes that while some locals speculate the death was a rape, there were no signs of sexual assault. Mrs. Oliver then introduces Poirot to Mrs. Reynolds, Joyce's mother, who is a slow-minded woman dressed in black mourning clothes.

Mrs. Reynolds expresses her grief and suspicion regarding the death, suggesting that a man might have broken in through a window to kill her daughter by holding her head underwater; she dismisses the possibility of a female killer. When Poirot asks about Joyce's claims of witnessing a murder, Mrs. Reynolds believes her daughter was merely joking or mixing things up, as she does not believe Joyce could have seen such an event. Mrs. Oliver adds that other children at the party likely laughed at Joyce or thought she was making things up to avoid encouraging her.

Poirot attempts to determine if Joyce had witnessed anything suspicious in the past, noting that a thirteen-year-old might claim to be "too young" to report something. Mrs. Reynolds, who was not at the party due to feeling unwell, mentions her other children: sixteen-year-old Anne and eleven-year-old Leo. Poirot subsequently speaks with Leo in the garden, where the boy is working on an airplane model. Leo dismisses Joyce's claims, stating that she frequently boasts and says anything to get attention.
